title: The World's Shortest YouTube Shorts Guide: From $0 To $30,000/mo

transcript.text: I've made over $220,000 in the past 6 months from just one of my Faceless Shorts channels. And if you want to do the same, you first need to start with an aged profile. These accounts have higher trust score and you can get one for just $8. Next, you need to choose a niche. The simpler your content, the more competitors you will have, and the faster those niches die out. So, complex branded niches are way easier to blow up and dominate over a long period of time. Here's a checklist how you can validate your niche when you choose one. Then you need a team because you're building this channel as a business, not as a side hustle. So instead of going on Upwork or Discord and working with overpriced freelancers, go on Telegram and search for communities of workers from these countries. These people are 50 70% cheaper yet have a European mentality and highquality standards. Once you have that, start building your content systems. This includes ideiation frameworks and prompts, scripting formats and guidelines, master sheets and on boarding processes for all of your employees and processes, and a distribution framework so you can make extra cash on Tik Tok, Facebook, Snapchat, and MSN. After that, optimize for audience overlap to make the YouTube algorithm glue your videos together in series and push them to your competitor's audiences. Once I did all of those steps myself, my seventh video blew up to 20 million views in 5 days. And in our best months, we hit 720 million views and made $53,000. And when we started sharing this with the Art of YouTube clients, one of them went from 7K a month to 20K a month in 30 days. And another one who just posted three videos already generated over 10 million views, getting 2.6 million on his last video in less than 18 hours. So, if you want a full in-depth 50-minute breakdown of exactly how the system works, DM me class on Instagram and I'll send it to
